pr.title: Match HSD_StateInitDirect and add matching HSD_InitComponent that needs data split
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Matched/added C for HSD_StateInitDirect in baselib displayfunc, introduced a C version of HSD_InitComponent behind NON_MATCHING while retaining the inline asm fallback, and propagated several newly identified SDK/HSD symbol names through asm callsites and functions.h. The PR also added displayfunc.o to e_files.mk and renamed call targets such as GXSetVtxDesc, GXSetVtxAttrFmt, GXSetCurrentMtx, HSD_StateInitTev, HSD_ClearVtxDesc, and HSD_SetupRenderMode across many existing assembly files.
what_changed: Added C implementation of HSD_StateInitDirect(int vtxfmt, u32 param_2) in src/sysdolphin/baselib/displayfunc.c.

HSD_StateInitDirect clears vertex descriptors, sets vertex attr formats for attrs 9 and 0xB, initializes TEV state, calls HSD_SetupRenderMode(param_2 | 0x28000000), enables GX vertex descriptors 9 and 0xB, and resets current matrix to 0.
Added displayfunc.o to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED in e_files.mk, allowing the new displayfunc C object to participate in the build.
Added a C HSD_InitComponent body under #ifdef NON_MATCHING while preserving the existing asm HSD_InitComponent under #else.

The HSD_InitComponent C candidate initializes OS, fills an HSD_VIStatus with rmode, black/vf/gamma/clear/update settings, calls HSD_VIInit with FrameBuffer[0..2], then runs HSD_GXInit, HSD_DVDInit, HSD_IDSetup, VIWaitForRetrace, HSD_ObjInit, func_803881E4, and sets init_done = TRUE.
Renamed func_8033BF00 to GXSetVtxDesc and func_8033C414 to GXSetVtxAttrFmt in asm/dolphin/gx/GXFifo.s.
Renamed func_80341510 callsites to GXSetCurrentMtx, including GXInit and numerous melee/sysdolphin asm files.
Renamed func_803624D8 to HSD_StateInitTev and func_8036C244 callsites to HSD_ClearVtxDesc.

Updated include/functions.h comments/prototypes to reflect newly identified symbols including GXSetVtxDesc, GXSetVtxAttrFmt, GXSetCurrentMtx, HSD_StateInitTev, HSD_ClearVtxDesc, and HSD_StateInitDirect.
smart_moves: The PR paired a new C match with broad symbol propagation, making existing assembly more readable without changing instruction bytes at callsites.
HSD_StateInitDirect was expressed as a small, high-level wrapper over already-identified GX/HSD primitives, which made the function intent clear and helped validate the renamed dependencies.
The HSD_InitComponent work was isolated behind NON_MATCHING rather than replacing the working asm path, preserving build correctness while documenting the likely C structure.
Adding displayfunc.o to e_files.mk in the same PR avoided leaving the matched C function disconnected from the build.
Renaming SDK-like GX functions to their official-style names made repeated rendering setup sequences across unrelated game systems easier to recognize.
decomp_lessons: When a low-level SDK/HSD primitive is identified, update all asm callsites immediately; the payoff is high because rendering setup code repeats throughout melee, lb, ft, gm, it, and sysdolphin files.

Small wrapper functions like HSD_StateInitDirect can often be matched by recognizing canonical GX setup order: clear descriptors, set attr formats, initialize TEV/render mode, enable descriptors, then set current matrix.
If a function is structurally understood but blocked by data placement or split issues, keep the C version under NON_MATCHING and retain asm as the matching build path.
Build-list changes such as adding an object to e_files.mk are part of the decomp match, not cleanup; include them with the C conversion so future matching attempts compile in the intended section.
Header/prototype updates should accompany symbol renames so subsequent C work can call the newly identified functions instead of relying on anonymous func_ labels.
naming_conventions: Dolphin GX SDK functions use GX-prefixed names such as GXSetVtxDesc, GXSetVtxAttrFmt, and GXSetCurrentMtx.
HAL/SysDolphin functions use HSD_-prefixed names such as HSD_StateInitDirect, HSD_StateInitTev, HSD_ClearVtxDesc, HSD_SetupRenderMode, and HSD_InitComponent.
Previously unknown labels in assembly were replaced directly at the .global/function-label site when the symbol identity was known, then corresponding bl callsites were updated.
Unresolved functions remained as func_ addresses or commented unknowns in functions.h, avoiding speculative renames beyond the evidence in this slice.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted one displayfunc routine to C while removing or reducing corresponding asm coverage in asm/sysdolphin/baselib/displayfunc.s.
Used exact symbol aliasing/renaming in assembly files to preserve existing branch offsets while improving semantic names.

Left HSD_InitComponent as inline asm for the matching path and placed the C equivalent behind #ifdef NON_MATCHING, indicating the source is understood but not yet safely linkable/matching due to a data split issue noted in the PR title.
Matched HSD_StateInitDirect as a straightforward ordered call sequence to HSD_ClearVtxDesc, GXSetVtxAttrFmt, HSD_StateInitTev, HSD_SetupRenderMode, GXSetVtxDesc, and GXSetCurrentMtx.
Propagated new names through many asm callsites instead of waiting for each caller to be decompiled, improving searchability and future reverse-engineering context.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that remove NON_MATCHING from HSD_InitComponent or resolve the data split blocker.
Search for callers of HSD_StateInitDirect to identify repeated GX setup patterns suitable for C conversion.
Search for remaining func_8033BF00, func_8033C414, func_80341510, func_803624D8, func_8036C244, and func_803738D0 references after this PR.
Inspect later header cleanup around GXSetVtxDesc, GXSetVtxAttrFmt, and GXSetCurrentMtx for proper typed prototypes.
Look for displayfunc.o section/order changes related to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED and baselib data splitting.
